title: coccothrinax miraguama subsp. miraguama
intro: Coccothrinax miraguama subsp. miraguama, also known as the Cuban thatch palm, is a plant species native to Cuba, characterized by its small stature and distinctive palm fronds. The plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. Consuming Coccothrinax miraguama subsp. miraguama is said to provide various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving digestive issues. The herbal preparations of this plant, including teas and infusions, are typically made by drying and crushing the leaves, which can be used to create medicinal remedies.
